This stage sits at the boundary between configuration loading and the rest of the running system: it exposes effective configuration through management APIs, persists user-initiated edits, and maintains a few editable state surfaces outside the main config stack. In the app server, config_processor.rs implements the JSON-RPC endpoints for reading and writing config, toggling runtime features, answering capability questions derived from config, and refreshing caches after changes. It delegates durable mutation work to config_manager_service.rs, which wraps ConfigManager with validation, optimistic concurrency checks, user-layer-only writes, and reporting about layer precedence, origins, and overrides when a write is shadowed.

At the persistence layer, core/src/config/edit.rs turns high-level mutations into atomic edits on config.toml, while document_helpers.rs performs the low-level TOML table/item transformations needed to preserve editable structure, especially for MCP servers and disabled-tool records. config_errors.rs converts configuration load failures into protocol-friendly JSON-RPC errors, including special extraction of cloud bundle failures. external_agent_config.rs imports settings and assets from an external agent into Codex-managed locations during onboarding. personality_migration.rs performs a one-time default-personality write for existing users, and app-server-daemon/src/settings.rs separately stores daemon-local settings such as remote-control enablement.
